intro: DEVICES and equipment used in gastroenterological care.
reading: GI devices include drainage and feeding systems, ostomy appliances, and diagnostic scopes. Interpreters should note the distinction between NG tube (inserted through the nose) and G tube (surgically placed through the abdomen), as well as ostomy bag terminology used in post-surgical care discussions.

terms.example_en: The nurse demonstrated how to empty and change the stoma bag.
terms.example_ru: Медсестра показала, как опустошать и менять мешок для стомы.

terms.example_en: The patient was unable to eat and required a feeding tube.
terms.example_ru: Пациент не мог питаться самостоятельно, поэтому ему потребовался зонд для кормления.
terms.note: General umbrella term; merged 2 source entries with different translations. Specific types: NG tube, G tube — see separate entries

terms.example_en: The patient needed a bedpan during the night.
terms.note: 'Утка' (urinal-type) is informal; 'судно' is the standard medical term

terms.example_en: The NG tube was inserted through the nose to drain stomach contents.
terms.example_ru: Назогастральный зонд ввели через нос для откачивания содержимого желудка.

terms.example_en: The G tube is placed directly into the stomach through the abdominal wall.
terms.example_ru: Гастростомическая трубка вводится непосредственно в желудок через брюшную стенку.
terms.note: Distinct from NG tube — surgically placed, not passed through the nose

terms.example_en: The JP drain was removed on the third day after surgery.
terms.example_ru: Дренаж Джексона–Пратта удалили на третий день после операции.

terms.example_en: The scope was passed through the mouth to examine the esophagus.
terms.example_ru: Эндоскоп ввели через рот для осмотра пищевода.
terms.note: Source: 'a scope ; зонд' — in GI context 'scope' means endoscope, not a generic probe
